    38→        required: true
    39→        type: string
    40→      plan_backend:
    41→        description: "Plan backend type (github or planned_pr)"
    42→        required: false
    43→        type: string
    44→        default: "github"
    45→  workflow_call:
    46→    inputs:
    47→      plan_id:
    48→        description: "Plan identifier to implement"
    49→        required: true
    50→        type: string
    51→      submitted_by:
    52→        description: "GitHub username of the person who submitted the issue"
    53→        required: true
    54→        type: string
    55→      distinct_id:
    56→        description: "Unique identifier for run discovery (base36)"
    57→        required: false
    58→        type: string
    59→        default: "called"
    60→      plan_title:
    61→        description: "Plan title for workflow run display"
    62→        required: true
    63→        type: string
    64→      branch_name:
    65→        description: "Branch name for implementation (created by submit command)"
    66→        required: true
    67→        type: string
    68→      pr_number:
    69→        description: "PR number for implementation (created by submit command)"
    70→        required: true
    71→        type: string
    72→      model_name:
    73→        description: "Claude model to use for implementation"
    74→        required: false
    75→        type: string
    76→        default: "claude-opus-4-6"
    77→      base_branch:
    78→        description: "Base branch for the implementation PR (may be stacked, not always trunk)"
    79→        required: true
    80→        type: string
    81→      plan_backend:
    82→        description: "Plan backend type (github or planned_pr)"
    83→        required: false
    84→        type: string
    85→        default: "github"
    86→
    87→concurrency:
    88→  group: implement-plan-${{ inputs.plan_id }}
    89→  cancel-in-progress: true
    90→
    91→jobs:
    92→  implement:
    93→    if: vars.CLAUDE_ENABLED != 'false'
    94→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    95→    timeout-minutes: 180
    96→    permissions:
    97→      contents: write
    98→      pull-requests: write
    99→      issues: write
   100→    env:
   101→      ERK_PLAN_BACKEND: ${{ inputs.plan_backend }}
   102→    steps:
   103→      - name: Validate required secrets
   104→        env:
   105→          ERK_PAT: ${{ secrets.ERK_QUEUE_GH_PAT }}
   106→        run: |
   107→          if [ -z "$ERK_PAT" ]; then
   108→            echo "::error title=Missing Secret::ERK_QUEUE_GH_PAT secret is not configured or has expired. Add a PAT with 'repo' scope at: Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions"
   109→            exit 1
   110→          fi
   111→
   112→      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
   113→        with:
   114→          token: ${{ secrets.ERK_QUEUE_GH_PAT }}
   115→          fetch-depth: 0
   116→
   117→      - uses: ./.github/actions/erk-remote-setup
   118→        with:
   119→          erk-pat: ${{ secrets.ERK_QUEUE_GH_PAT }}
   120→          anthropic-api-key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
   121→          claude-code-oauth-token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
   122→
   123→      - name: Detect trunk branch
   124→        id: trunk
   125→        run: |
   126→          result=$(erk exec detect-trunk-branch)
   127→          echo "trunk_branch=$(echo "$result" | jq -r '.trunk_branch')" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   128→
   129→      - name: Set branch and PR from inputs
   130→        id: find_pr
   131→        run: |
   132→          echo "branch_name=${{ inputs.branch_name }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   133→          echo "pr_number=${{ inputs.pr_number }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   134→          echo "pr_exists=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   135→          echo "Using branch: ${{ inputs.branch_name }}"
   136→          echo "Using PR: #${{ inputs.pr_number }}"
   137→
   138→      - name: Checkout implementation branch
   139→        env:
   140→          BRANCH_NAME: ${{ steps.find_pr.outputs.branch_name }}
   141→          PLAN_ID: ${{ inputs.plan_id }}
   142→          SUBMITTED_BY: ${{ inputs.submitted_by }}
   143→          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
   144→          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
   145→          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
   146→        run: |
   147→          git fetch origin "$BRANCH_NAME"
   148→          git checkout "$BRANCH_NAME"
   149→          rm -rf .erk/impl-context
   150→          erk exec create-impl-context-from-plan "$PLAN_ID"
   151→          if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
   152→            if [ "$ERK_PLAN_BACKEND" = "planned_pr" ]; then
   153→              gh pr comment "$PLAN_ID" --body "Could not fetch plan content for plan #$PLAN_ID."
   154→            else
   155→              gh issue comment "$PLAN_ID" --body "Could not fetch plan content for plan #$PLAN_ID."
   156→            fi
   157→            exit 1
   158→          fi
   159→          git config user.name "$SUBMITTED_BY"
   160→          git config user.email "$SUBMITTED_BY@users.noreply.github.com"
   161→          git add -f .erk/impl-context
   162→          if [[ -n $(git status --porcelain) ]]; then
   163→            git commit -m "Update plan for plan #$PLAN_ID (rerun)"
   164→            git push origin "$BRANCH_NAME"
   165→          fi
   166→          echo "Checked out branch: $BRANCH_NAME"
   167→
   168→      - name: Post workflow started comment
   169→        env:
   170→          PLAN_ID: ${{ inputs.plan_id }}
   171→          BRANCH_NAME: ${{ steps.find_pr.outputs.branch_name }}
   172→          PR_NUMBER: ${{ inputs.pr_number }}
   173→          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
   174→        run: |
   175→          erk exec post-workflow-started-comment \
   176→            --plan-id "$PLAN_ID" \
   177→            --branch-name "$BRANCH_NAME" \
   178→            --pr-number "$PR_NUMBER" \
   179→            --run-id "${{ github.run_id }}" \
   180→            --run-url "${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}" \
   181→            --repository "${{ github.repository }}"
   182→
   183→      - name: Add remote execution note to PR
   184→        env:
   185→          PR_NUMBER: ${{ inputs.pr_number }}
   186→          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
   187→          WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
   188→          WORKFLOW_RUN_URL: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}
   189→        run: |
   190→          erk exec add-remote-execution-note \
   191→            --pr-number "$PR_NUMBER" \
   192→            --run-id "$WORKFLOW_RUN_ID" \
   193→            --run-url "$WORKFLOW_RUN_URL"
   194→
   195→      - name: Set up implementation folder
   196→        run: |
   197→          cp -r .erk/impl-context .impl
   198→          echo "Copied .erk/impl-context/ to .impl/"
   199→          cat > .impl/run-info.json <<EOF
   200→          {
   201→            "run_id": "${{ github.run_id }}",
   202→            "run_url": "${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}"
   203→          }
   204→          EOF
   205→          echo "Created .impl/run-info.json"
   206→
   207→      - name: Remove plan staging dirs from git tracking
   208→        env:
   209→          BRANCH_NAME: ${{ steps.find_pr.outputs.branch_name }}
   210→          SUBMITTED_BY: ${{ inputs.submitted_by }}
   211→        run: |
   212→          NEEDS_CLEANUP=false
   213→          git config user.name "$SUBMITTED_BY"
   214→          git config user.email "$SUBMITTED_BY@users.noreply.github.com"
   215→          if [ -d .erk/impl-context/ ]; then
   216→            git rm -rf .erk/impl-context/
   217→            NEEDS_CLEANUP=true
   218→          fi
   219→          if [ "$NEEDS_CLEANUP" = true ]; then
   220→            git commit -m "Remove plan staging dirs before implementation"
   221→            git push origin "$BRANCH_NAME"
   222→            echo "Removed plan staging dirs from git tracking (content preserved in .impl/)"
   223→          fi
   224→
   225→      - name: Save pre-implementation HEAD
   226→        id: pre_impl
   227→        run: echo "head=$(git rev-parse HEAD)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   228→
   229→      - name: Run implementation
   230→        id: implement
   231→        run: |
   232→          set +e
   233→          claude --print \
   234→            --model ${{ inputs.model_name }} \
   235→            --output-format stream-json \
   236→            --dangerously-skip-permissions \
   237→            --verbose \
   238→            "/erk:plan-implement $PLAN_ID"
   239→          EXIT_CODE=$?
   240→          echo "exit_code=$EXIT_CODE" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   241→          if [ $EXIT_CODE -eq 0 ]; then
   242→            echo "implementation_success=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   243→          else
   244→            echo "implementation_success=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   245→          fi
   246→          exit 0
   247→        env:
   248→          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
   249→          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
   250→          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
   251→          PR_NUMBER: ${{ inputs.pr_number }}
   252→          PLAN_ID: ${{ inputs.plan_id }}
   253→
   254→      - name: Capture session ID
   255→        id: session
   256→        if: always()
   257→        run: |
   258→          if OUTPUT=$(erk exec capture-session-info --path "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE" 2>/dev/null); then
   259→            eval "$OUTPUT"
   260→            echo "session_id=$SESSION_ID" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
   261→            echo "session_file=$SESSION_FILE" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
   262→            echo "Captured session ID: $SESSION_ID"
   263→            echo "Session file: $SESSION_FILE"
   264→          else
   265→            echo "No session found for: $GITHUB_WORKSPACE"
   266→          fi
   267→
   268→      - name: Upload session to gist and update plan header
   269→        if: always() && steps.session.outputs.session_id
   270→        env:
   271→          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.ERK_QUEUE_GH_PAT }}
   272→          PLAN_ID: ${{ inputs.plan_id }}
   273→          SESSION_ID: ${{ steps.session.outputs.session_id }}
   274→          SESSION_FILE: ${{ steps.session.outputs.session_file }}
   275→        run: |
   276→          erk exec upload-session \
   277→            --session-file "$SESSION_FILE" \
   278→            --session-id "$SESSION_ID" \
   279→            --source remote \
   280→            --plan-id "$PLAN_ID"
   281→
   282→      - name: Update plan header with remote impl info
   283→        if: always() && steps.session.outputs.session_id
   284→        continue-on-error: true
   285→        env:
   286→          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.ERK_QUEUE_GH_PAT }}
   287→          PLAN_ID: ${{ inputs.plan_id }}
   288→          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
   289→          SESSION_ID: ${{ steps.session.outputs.session_id }}
   290→          BRANCH_NAME: ${{ inputs.branch_name }}
   291→        run: |
   292→          TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S+00:00")
   293→          erk exec update-plan-header "$PLAN_ID" \
   294→            "last_remote_impl_at=$TIMESTAMP" \
   295→            "last_remote_impl_run_id=$RUN_ID" \
   296→            "last_remote_impl_session_id=$SESSION_ID" \
   297→            "branch_name=$BRANCH_NAME"
   298→
   299→      - name: Handle implementation outcome
   300→        id: handle_outcome
   301→        if: steps.implement.outputs.implementation_success == 'true'
   302→        env:
   303→          BASE_BRANCH: ${{ inputs.base_branch }}
   304→          PLAN_ID: ${{ inputs.plan_id }}
   305→          PLAN_TITLE: ${{ inputs.plan_title }}
   306→          PR_NUMBER: ${{ inputs.pr_number }}
   307→          PRE_IMPL_HEAD: ${{ steps.pre_impl.outputs.head }}
   308→          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
   309→        run: |
   310→          # Check for uncommitted changes (staged or unstaged) excluding .erk/impl-context/ and .impl/
   311→          UNCOMMITTED=$(git status --porcelain | grep -v '^\s*D.*\.erk/impl-context/' | grep -v '\.impl/' || true)
   312→
   313→          # Check for new commits since pre-implementation
   314→          CURRENT_HEAD=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
   315→          NEW_COMMITS="false"
   316→          if [ "$CURRENT_HEAD" != "$PRE_IMPL_HEAD" ]; then
   317→            NEW_COMMITS="true"
   318→            echo "New commits detected since pre-implementation HEAD"
   319→          fi
   320→
   321→          # Has changes if either condition is true
   322→          if [ -z "$UNCOMMITTED" ] && [ "$NEW_COMMITS" = "false" ]; then
   323→            echo "No code changes detected, handling gracefully..."
   324→
   325→            # Gather diagnostic info
   326→            git fetch origin "$BASE_BRANCH" --quiet
   327→            BEHIND_COUNT=$(git rev-list HEAD.."origin/$BASE_BRANCH" --count 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
   328→            RECENT_COMMITS=$(git log HEAD.."origin/$BASE_BRANCH" --oneline --max-count=5 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   329→
   330→            # Call exec command to handle no-changes scenario
   331→            erk exec handle-no-changes \
   332→              --pr-number "$PR_NUMBER" \
   333→              --plan-id "$PLAN_ID" \
   334→              --behind-count "$BEHIND_COUNT" \
   335→              --base-branch "$BASE_BRANCH" \
   336→              --original-title "$PLAN_TITLE" \
   337→              --recent-commits "$RECENT_COMMITS" \
   338→              --run-url "${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}"
   339→
   340→            echo "has_changes=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   341→          else
   342→            echo "Implementation produced changes:"
   343→            if [ -n "$UNCOMMITTED" ]; then
   344→              echo "Uncommitted changes:"
   345→              echo "$UNCOMMITTED"
   346→            fi
   347→            if [ "$NEW_COMMITS" = "true" ]; then
   348→              echo "New commits since $PRE_IMPL_HEAD:"
   349→              git log --oneline "$PRE_IMPL_HEAD"..HEAD
   350→            fi
   351→            echo "has_changes=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   352→          fi
   353→
   354→      - name: Submit branch with proper commit message
   355→        id: submit
   356→        continue-on-error: true
   357→        if: steps.implement.outputs.implementation_success == 'true' && steps.handle_outcome.outputs.has_changes == 'true'
   358→        env:
   359→          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
   360→          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
   361→          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
   362→          PLAN_ID: ${{ inputs.plan_id }}
   363→          BRANCH_NAME: ${{ steps.find_pr.outputs.branch_name }}
   364→          SUBMITTED_BY: ${{ inputs.submitted_by }}
   365→        run: |
   366→          git config user.name "$SUBMITTED_BY"
   367→          git config user.email "$SUBMITTED_BY@users.noreply.github.com"
   368→          claude --print \
   369→            --model ${{ inputs.model_name }} \
   370→            --output-format stream-json \
   371→            --dangerously-skip-permissions \
   372→            --verbose \
   373→            "/erk:git-pr-push Implement plan #$PLAN_ID"
   374→          echo "impl_sha=$(git rev-parse HEAD)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   375→
   376→      - name: Handle merge conflicts if push failed
   377→        id: handle_conflicts
   378→        if: steps.submit.outcome == 'failure'
   379→        env:
   380→          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
   381→          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
   382→          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
   383→        run: |
   384→          erk exec rebase-with-conflict-resolution \
   385→            --target-branch "${{ inputs.base_branch }}" \
   386→            --branch-name "${{ steps.find_pr.outputs.branch_name }}" \
   387→            --model "${{ inputs.model_name }}"
   388→
   389→      - name: Mark PR ready for review
   390→        if: steps.implement.outputs.implementation_success == 'true' && steps.handle_outcome.outputs.has_changes == 'true' && (steps.submit.outcome == 'success' || steps.handle_conflicts.outcome == 'success')
   391→        env:
   392→          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
   393→          BRANCH_NAME: ${{ steps.find_pr.outputs.branch_name }}
   394→        run: |
   395→          gh pr ready "$BRANCH_NAME"
   396→          echo "PR marked ready for review"
   397→
   398→      - name: Clean up plan staging dirs after implementation
   399→        if: steps.implement.outputs.implementation_success == 'true' && steps.handle_outcome.outputs.has_changes == 'true' && (steps.submit.outcome == 'success' || steps.handle_conflicts.outcome == 'success')
   400→        env:
   401→          BRANCH_NAME: ${{ steps.find_pr.outputs.branch_name }}
   402→          SUBMITTED_BY: ${{ inputs.submitted_by }}
   403→        run: |
   404→          git fetch origin "$BRANCH_NAME"
   405→          git reset --hard "origin/$BRANCH_NAME"
   406→          NEEDS_CLEANUP=false
   407→          git config user.name "$SUBMITTED_BY"
   408→          git config user.email "$SUBMITTED_BY@users.noreply.github.com"
   409→          if [ -d .erk/impl-context/ ]; then
   410→            git rm -rf .erk/impl-context/
   411→            NEEDS_CLEANUP=true
   412→          fi
   413→          if [ "$NEEDS_CLEANUP" = true ]; then
   414→            git commit -m "Remove plan staging dirs after implementation"
   415→            git push origin "$BRANCH_NAME"
   416→            echo "Cleaned up plan staging dirs"
   417→          fi
   418→
   419→      - name: Update PR body with implementation summary
   420→        continue-on-error: true
   421→        if: steps.implement.outputs.implementation_success == 'true' && steps.handle_outcome.outputs.has_changes == 'true' && (steps.submit.outcome == 'success' || steps.handle_conflicts.outcome == 'success')
   422→        env:
   423→          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
   424→          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
   425→          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
   426→        run: |
   427→          PLANNED_PR_FLAG=""
   428→          if [ "${{ inputs.plan_backend }}" = "planned_pr" ]; then
   429→            PLANNED_PR_FLAG="--planned-pr"
   430→          fi
   431→          erk exec ci-update-pr-body \
   432→            --plan-id "${{ inputs.plan_id }}" \
   433→            --run-id "${{ github.run_id }}" \
   434→            --run-url "${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}" \
   435→            $PLANNED_PR_FLAG
   436→
   437→      - name: Trigger CI workflows
   438→        if: steps.implement.outputs.implementation_success == 'true' && steps.handle_outcome.outputs.has_changes == 'true' && (steps.submit.outcome == 'success' || steps.handle_conflicts.outcome == 'success')
   439→        env:
   440→          BRANCH_NAME: ${{ steps.find_pr.outputs.branch_name }}
   441→          SUBMITTED_BY: ${{ inputs.submitted_by }}
   442→        run: |
   443→          git config user.name "$SUBMITTED_BY"
   444→          git config user.email "$SUBMITTED_BY@users.noreply.github.com"
   445→          git fetch origin "$BRANCH_NAME"
   446→          git reset --hard "origin/$BRANCH_NAME"
   447→          git commit --allow-empty -m "Trigger CI workflows"
   448→          git push origin "$BRANCH_NAME"
   449→          echo "CI workflows triggered via push event"
   450→
   451→      - name: Update lifecycle stage to implemented
   452→        if: steps.implement.outputs.implementation_success == 'true' && steps.handle_outcome.outputs.has_changes == 'true' && (steps.submit.outcome == 'success' || steps.handle_conflicts.outcome == 'success')
   453→        run: |
   454→          erk exec impl-signal submitted
   455→
   456→      - name: Fail workflow if implementation failed
   457→        if: steps.implement.outputs.implementation_success != 'true'
   458→        run: |
   459→          echo "Implementation failed with exit code: ${{ steps.implement.outputs.exit_code }}"
   460→          exit 1
   461→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

file.content:
name: plan-implement
run-name: "${{ inputs.plan_id }}:${{ inputs.distinct_id }}"

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      plan_id:
        description: "Plan identifier to implement"
        required: true
        type: string
      submitted_by:
        description: "GitHub username of the person who submitted the issue"
        required: true
        type: string
      distinct_id:
        description: "Unique identifier for run discovery (base36)"
        required: true
        type: string
      plan_title:
        description: "Plan title for workflow run display"
        required: true
        type: string
      branch_name:
        description: "Branch name for implementation (created by submit command)"
        required: true
        type: string
      pr_number:
        description: "PR number for implementation (created by submit command)"
        required: true
        type: string
      model_name:
        description: "Claude model to use for implementation"
        required: false
        type: string
        default: "claude-opus-4-6"
      base_branch:
        description: "Base branch for the implementation PR (may be stacked, not always trunk)"
        required: true
        type: string
      plan_backend:
        description: "Plan backend type (github or planned_pr)"
        required: false
        type: string
        default: "github"
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      plan_id:
        description: "Plan identifier to implement"
        required: true
        type: string
      submitted_by:
        description: "GitHub username of the person who submitted the issue"
        required: true
        type: string
      distinct_id:
        description: "Unique identifier for run discovery (base36)"
        required: false
        type: string
        default: "called"
      plan_title:
        description: "Plan title for workflow run display"
        required: true
        type: string
      branch_name:
        description: "Branch name for implementation (created by submit command)"
        required: true
        type: string
      pr_number:
        description: "PR number for implementation (created by submit command)"
        required: true
        type: string
      model_name:
        description: "Claude model to use for implementation"
        required: false
        type: string
        default: "claude-opus-4-6"
      base_branch:
        description: "Base branch for the implementation PR (may be stacked, not always trunk)"
        required: true
        type: string
      plan_backend:
        description: "Plan backend type (github or planned_pr)"
        required: false
        type: string
        default: "github"

concurrency:
  group: implement-plan-${{ inputs.plan_id }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  implement:
    if: vars.CLAUDE_ENABLED != 'false'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 180
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      issues: write
    env:
      ERK_PLAN_BACKEND: ${{ inputs.plan_backend }}
    steps:
      - name: Validate required secrets
        env:
          ERK_PAT: ${{ secrets.ERK_QUEUE_GH_PAT }}
        run: |
          if [ -z "$ERK_PAT" ]; then
            echo "::error title=Missing Secret::ERK_QUEUE_GH_PAT secret is not configured or has expired. Add a PAT with 'repo' scope at: Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions"
            exit 1
          fi

      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.ERK_QUEUE_GH_PAT }}
          fetch-depth: 0

      - uses: ./.github/actions/erk-remote-setup
        with:
          erk-pat: ${{ secrets.ERK_QUEUE_GH_PAT }}
          anthropic-api-key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
          claude-code-oauth-token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}

      - name: Detect trunk branch
        id: trunk
        run: |
          result=$(erk exec detect-trunk-branch)
          echo "trunk_branch=$(echo "$result" | jq -r '.trunk_branch')" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Set branch and PR from inputs
        id: find_pr
        run: |
          echo "branch_name=${{ inputs.branch_name }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "pr_number=${{ inputs.pr_number }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "pr_exists=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "Using branch: ${{ inputs.branch_name }}"
          echo "Using PR: #${{ inputs.pr_number }}"

      - name: Checkout implementation branch
        env:
          BRANCH_NAME: ${{ steps.find_pr.outputs.branch_name }}
          PLAN_ID: ${{ inputs.plan_id }}
          SUBMITTED_BY: ${{ inputs.submitted_by }}
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          git fetch origin "$BRANCH_NAME"
          git checkout "$BRANCH_NAME"
          rm -rf .erk/impl-context
          erk exec create-impl-context-from-plan "$PLAN_ID"
          if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
            if [ "$ERK_PLAN_BACKEND" = "planned_pr" ]; then
              gh pr comment "$PLAN_ID" --body "Could not fetch plan content for plan #$PLAN_ID."
            else
              gh issue comment "$PLAN_ID" --body "Could not fetch plan content for plan #$PLAN_ID."
            fi
            exit 1
          fi
          git config user.name "$SUBMITTED_BY"
          git config user.email "$SUBMITTED_BY@users.noreply.github.com"
          git add -f .erk/impl-context
          if [[ -n $(git status --porcelain) ]]; then
            git commit -m "Update plan for plan #$PLAN_ID (rerun)"
            git push origin "$BRANCH_NAME"
          fi
          echo "Checked out branch: $BRANCH_NAME"

      - name: Post workflow started comment
        env:
          PLAN_ID: ${{ inputs.plan_id }}
          BRANCH_NAME: ${{ steps.find_pr.outputs.branch_name }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ inputs.pr_number }}
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
        run: |
          erk exec post-workflow-started-comment \
            --plan-id "$PLAN_ID" \
            --branch-name "$BRANCH_NAME" \
            --pr-number "$PR_NUMBER" \
            --run-id "${{ github.run_id }}" \
            --run-url "${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}" \
            --repository "${{ github.repository }}"

      - name: Add remote execution note to PR
        env:
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ inputs.pr_number }}
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
          WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          WORKFLOW_RUN_URL: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          erk exec add-remote-execution-note \
            --pr-number "$PR_NUMBER" \
            --run-id "$WORKFLOW_RUN_ID" \
            --run-url "$WORKFLOW_RUN_URL"

      - name: Set up implementation folder
        run: |
          cp -r .erk/impl-context .impl
          echo "Copied .erk/impl-context/ to .impl/"
          cat > .impl/run-info.json <<EOF
          {
            "run_id": "${{ github.run_id }}",
            "run_url": "${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}"
          }
          EOF
          echo "Created .impl/run-info.json"

      - name: Remove plan staging dirs from git tracking
        env:
          BRANCH_NAME: ${{ steps.find_pr.outputs.branch_name }}
          SUBMITTED_BY: ${{ inputs.submitted_by }}
        run: |
          NEEDS_CLEANUP=false
          git config user.name "$SUBMITTED_BY"
          git config user.email "$SUBMITTED_BY@users.noreply.github.com"
          if [ -d .erk/impl-context/ ]; then
            git rm -rf .erk/impl-context/
            NEEDS_CLEANUP=true
          fi
          if [ "$NEEDS_CLEANUP" = true ]; then
            git commit -m "Remove plan staging dirs before implementation"
            git push origin "$BRANCH_NAME"
            echo "Removed plan staging dirs from git tracking (content preserved in .impl/)"
          fi

      - name: Save pre-implementation HEAD
        id: pre_impl
        run: echo "head=$(git rev-parse HEAD)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Run implementation
        id: implement
        run: |
          set +e
          claude --print \
            --model ${{ inputs.model_name }} \
            --output-format stream-json \
            --dangerously-skip-permissions \
            --verbose \
            "/erk:plan-implement $PLAN_ID"
          EXIT_CODE=$?
          echo "exit_code=$EXIT_CODE" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          if [ $EXIT_CODE -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "implementation_success=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "implementation_success=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi
          exit 0
        env:
          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ inputs.pr_number }}
          PLAN_ID: ${{ inputs.plan_id }}

      - name: Capture session ID
        id: session
        if: always()
        run: |
          if OUTPUT=$(erk exec capture-session-info --path "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE" 2>/dev/null); then
            eval "$OUTPUT"
            echo "session_id=$SESSION_ID" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "session_file=$SESSION_FILE" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "Captured session ID: $SESSION_ID"
            echo "Session file: $SESSION_FILE"
          else
            echo "No session found for: $GITHUB_WORKSPACE"
          fi

      - name: Upload session to gist and update plan header
        if: always() && steps.session.outputs.session_id
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.ERK_QUEUE_GH_PAT }}
          PLAN_ID: ${{ inputs.plan_id }}
          SESSION_ID: ${{ steps.session.outputs.session_id }}
          SESSION_FILE: ${{ steps.session.outputs.session_file }}
        run: |
          erk exec upload-session \
            --session-file "$SESSION_FILE" \
            --session-id "$SESSION_ID" \
            --source remote \
            --plan-id "$PLAN_ID"

      - name: Update plan header with remote impl info
        if: always() && steps.session.outputs.session_id
        continue-on-error: true
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.ERK_QUEUE_GH_PAT }}
          PLAN_ID: ${{ inputs.plan_id }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          SESSION_ID: ${{ steps.session.outputs.session_id }}
          BRANCH_NAME: ${{ inputs.branch_name }}
        run: |
          TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S+00:00")
          erk exec update-plan-header "$PLAN_ID" \
            "last_remote_impl_at=$TIMESTAMP" \
            "last_remote_impl_run_id=$RUN_ID" \
            "last_remote_impl_session_id=$SESSION_ID" \
            "branch_name=$BRANCH_NAME"

      - name: Handle implementation outcome
        id: handle_outcome
        if: steps.implement.outputs.implementation_success == 'true'
        env:
          BASE_BRANCH: ${{ inputs.base_branch }}
          PLAN_ID: ${{ inputs.plan_id }}
          PLAN_TITLE: ${{ inputs.plan_title }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ inputs.pr_number }}
          PRE_IMPL_HEAD: ${{ steps.pre_impl.outputs.head }}
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
        run: |
          # Check for uncommitted changes (staged or unstaged) excluding .erk/impl-context/ and .impl/
          UNCOMMITTED=$(git status --porcelain | grep -v '^\s*D.*\.erk/impl-context/' | grep -v '\.impl/' || true)

          # Check for new commits since pre-implementation
          CURRENT_HEAD=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
          NEW_COMMITS="false"
          if [ "$CURRENT_HEAD" != "$PRE_IMPL_HEAD" ]; then
            NEW_COMMITS="true"
            echo "New commits detected since pre-implementation HEAD"
          fi

          # Has changes if either condition is true
          if [ -z "$UNCOMMITTED" ] && [ "$NEW_COMMITS" = "false" ]; then
            echo "No code changes detected, handling gracefully..."

            # Gather diagnostic info
            git fetch origin "$BASE_BRANCH" --quiet
            BEHIND_COUNT=$(git rev-list HEAD.."origin/$BASE_BRANCH" --count 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
            RECENT_COMMITS=$(git log HEAD.."origin/$BASE_BRANCH" --oneline --max-count=5 2>/dev/null || echo "")

            # Call exec command to handle no-changes scenario
            erk exec handle-no-changes \
              --pr-number "$PR_NUMBER" \
              --plan-id "$PLAN_ID" \
              --behind-count "$BEHIND_COUNT" \
              --base-branch "$BASE_BRANCH" \
              --original-title "$PLAN_TITLE" \
              --recent-commits "$RECENT_COMMITS" \
              --run-url "${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}"

            echo "has_changes=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "Implementation produced changes:"
            if [ -n "$UNCOMMITTED" ]; then
              echo "Uncommitted changes:"
              echo "$UNCOMMITTED"
            fi
            if [ "$NEW_COMMITS" = "true" ]; then
              echo "New commits since $PRE_IMPL_HEAD:"
              git log --oneline "$PRE_IMPL_HEAD"..HEAD
            fi
            echo "has_changes=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

      - name: Submit branch with proper commit message
        id: submit
        continue-on-error: true
        if: steps.implement.outputs.implementation_success == 'true' && steps.handle_outcome.outputs.has_changes == 'true'
        env:
          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
          PLAN_ID: ${{ inputs.plan_id }}
          BRANCH_NAME: ${{ steps.find_pr.outputs.branch_name }}
          SUBMITTED_BY: ${{ inputs.submitted_by }}
        run: |
          git config user.name "$SUBMITTED_BY"
          git config user.email "$SUBMITTED_BY@users.noreply.github.com"
          claude --print \
            --model ${{ inputs.model_name }} \
            --output-format stream-json \
            --dangerously-skip-permissions \
            --verbose \
            "/erk:git-pr-push Implement plan #$PLAN_ID"
          echo "impl_sha=$(git rev-parse HEAD)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Handle merge conflicts if push failed
        id: handle_conflicts
        if: steps.submit.outcome == 'failure'
        env:
          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
        run: |
          erk exec rebase-with-conflict-resolution \
            --target-branch "${{ inputs.base_branch }}" \
            --branch-name "${{ steps.find_pr.outputs.branch_name }}" \
            --model "${{ inputs.model_name }}"

      - name: Mark PR ready for review
        if: steps.implement.outputs.implementation_success == 'true' && steps.handle_outcome.outputs.has_changes == 'true' && (steps.submit.outcome == 'success' || steps.handle_conflicts.outcome == 'success')
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
          BRANCH_NAME: ${{ steps.find_pr.outputs.branch_name }}
        run: |
          gh pr ready "$BRANCH_NAME"
          echo "PR marked ready for review"

      - name: Clean up plan staging dirs after implementation
        if: steps.implement.outputs.implementation_success == 'true' && steps.handle_outcome.outputs.has_changes == 'true' && (steps.submit.outcome == 'success' || steps.handle_conflicts.outcome == 'success')
        env:
          BRANCH_NAME: ${{ steps.find_pr.outputs.branch_name }}
          SUBMITTED_BY: ${{ inputs.submitted_by }}
        run: |
          git fetch origin "$BRANCH_NAME"
          git reset --hard "origin/$BRANCH_NAME"
          NEEDS_CLEANUP=false
          git config user.name "$SUBMITTED_BY"
          git config user.email "$SUBMITTED_BY@users.noreply.github.com"
          if [ -d .erk/impl-context/ ]; then
            git rm -rf .erk/impl-context/
            NEEDS_CLEANUP=true
          fi
          if [ "$NEEDS_CLEANUP" = true ]; then
            git commit -m "Remove plan staging dirs after implementation"
            git push origin "$BRANCH_NAME"
            echo "Cleaned up plan staging dirs"
          fi

      - name: Update PR body with implementation summary
        continue-on-error: true
        if: steps.implement.outputs.implementation_success == 'true' && steps.handle_outcome.outputs.has_changes == 'true' && (steps.submit.outcome == 'success' || steps.handle_conflicts.outcome == 'success')
        env:
          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
        run: |
          PLANNED_PR_FLAG=""
          if [ "${{ inputs.plan_backend }}" = "planned_pr" ]; then
            PLANNED_PR_FLAG="--planned-pr"
          fi
          erk exec ci-update-pr-body \
            --plan-id "${{ inputs.plan_id }}" \
            --run-id "${{ github.run_id }}" \
            --run-url "${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}" \
            $PLANNED_PR_FLAG

      - name: Trigger CI workflows
        if: steps.implement.outputs.implementation_success == 'true' && steps.handle_outcome.outputs.has_changes == 'true' && (steps.submit.outcome == 'success' || steps.handle_conflicts.outcome == 'success')
        env:
          BRANCH_NAME: ${{ steps.find_pr.outputs.branch_name }}
          SUBMITTED_BY: ${{ inputs.submitted_by }}
        run: |
          git config user.name "$SUBMITTED_BY"
          git config user.email "$SUBMITTED_BY@users.noreply.github.com"
          git fetch origin "$BRANCH_NAME"
          git reset --hard "origin/$BRANCH_NAME"
          git commit --allow-empty -m "Trigger CI workflows"
          git push origin "$BRANCH_NAME"
          echo "CI workflows triggered via push event"

      - name: Update lifecycle stage to implemented
        if: steps.implement.outputs.implementation_success == 'true' && steps.handle_outcome.outputs.has_changes == 'true' && (steps.submit.outcome == 'success' || steps.handle_conflicts.outcome == 'success')
        run: |
          erk exec impl-signal submitted

      - name: Fail workflow if implementation failed
        if: steps.implement.outputs.implementation_success != 'true'
        run: |
          echo "Implementation failed with exit code: ${{ steps.implement.outputs.exit_code }}"
          exit 1

1→"""Fake git operations for testing.
     2→
     3→FakeGit is an in-memory implementation that accepts pre-configured state
     4→in its constructor. Construct instances directly with keyword arguments.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→from __future__ import annotations
     8→
     9→from collections.abc import Callable
    10→from pathlib import Path
    11→from typing import NamedTuple
    12→
    13→from erk_shared.gateway.git.abc import (
    14→    BranchDivergence,
    15→    BranchSyncInfo,
    16→    Git,
    17→    RebaseResult,
    18→    WorktreeInfo,
    19→)
    20→from erk_shared.gateway.git.analysis_ops.abc import GitAnalysisOps
    21→from erk_shared.gateway.git.analysis_ops.fake import FakeGitAnalysisOps
    22→from erk_shared.gateway.git.branch_ops.abc import GitBranchOps
    23→from erk_shared.gateway.git.branch_ops.fake import FakeGitBranchOps
    24→from erk_shared.gateway.git.branch_ops.types import BranchAlreadyExists
    25→from erk_shared.gateway.git.commit_ops.abc import GitCommitOps
    26→from erk_shared.gateway.git.commit_ops.fake import (
    27→    BranchCommitRecord,
    28→    CommitRecord,
    29→    FakeGitCommitOps,
    30→)
    31→from erk_shared.gateway.git.config_ops.abc import GitConfigOps
    32→from erk_shared.gateway.git.config_ops.fake import ConfigSetRecord, FakeGitConfigOps
    33→from erk_shared.gateway.git.rebase_ops.abc import GitRebaseOps
    34→from erk_shared.gateway.git.rebase_ops.fake import FakeGitRebaseOps
    35→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.abc import GitRemoteOps
    36→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.fake import FakeGitRemoteOps
    37→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.types import PullRebaseError, PushError
    38→from erk_shared.gateway.git.repo_ops.abc import GitRepoOps
    39→from erk_shared.gateway.git.repo_ops.fake import FakeGitRepoOps
    40→from erk_shared.gateway.git.status_ops.abc import GitStatusOps
    41→from erk_shared.gateway.git.status_ops.fake import FakeGitStatusOps
    42→from erk_shared.gateway.git.tag_ops.abc import GitTagOps
    43→from erk_shared.gateway.git.tag_ops.fake import FakeGitTagOps
    44→from erk_shared.gateway.git.worktree.abc import Worktree
    45→from erk_shared.gateway.git.worktree.fake import FakeWorktree
    46→
    47→
    48→class PushedBranch(NamedTuple):
    49→    """Record of a branch push operation.
    50→
    51→    Attributes:
    52→        remote: Remote name (e.g., "origin")
    53→        branch: Branch name that was pushed
    54→        set_upstream: Whether -u flag was used to set upstream tracking
    55→        force: Whether --force flag was used for force push
    56→    """
    57→
    58→    remote: str
    59→    branch: str
    60→    set_upstream: bool
    61→    force: bool
    62→
    63→
    64→class FakeGit(Git):
    65→    """In-memory fake implementation of git operations.
    66→
    67→    State Management:
    68→    -----------------
    69→    This fake maintains mutable state to simulate git's stateful behavior.
    70→    Operations like add_worktree, checkout_branch modify internal state.
    71→    State changes are visible to subsequent method calls within the same test.
    72→
    73→    When to Use Mutation:
    74→    --------------------
    75→    - Operations that simulate stateful external systems (git, databases)
    76→    - When tests need to verify sequences of operations
    77→    - When simulating side effects visible to production code
    78→
    79→    Constructor Injection:
    80→    ---------------------
    81→    All INITIAL state is provided via constructor (immutable after construction).
    82→    Runtime mutations occur through operation methods.
    83→    Tests should construct fakes with complete initial state.
    84→
    85→    Mutation Tracking:
    86→    -----------------
    87→    This fake tracks mutations for test assertions via read-only properties:
    88→    - deleted_branches: Branches deleted via delete_branch()
    89→    - added_worktrees: Worktrees added via add_worktree()
    90→    - removed_worktrees: Worktrees removed via remove_worktree()
    91→    - checked_out_branches: Branches checked out via checkout_branch()
    92→
    93→    Examples:
    94→    ---------
    95→        # Initial state via constructor
    96→        git_ops = FakeGit(
    97→            worktrees={repo: [WorktreeInfo(path=wt1, branch="main")]},
    98→            current_branches={wt1: "main"},
    99→            git_common_dirs={repo: repo / ".git"},
   100→        )
   101→
   102→        # Mutation through operation
   103→        git_ops.worktree.add_worktree(repo, wt2, branch="feature")
   104→
   105→        # Verify mutation
   106→        assert len(git_ops.worktree.list_worktrees(repo)) == 2
   107→        assert (wt2, "feature") in git_ops.added_worktrees
   108→
   109→        # Verify sequence of operations
   110→        git_ops.checkout_branch(repo, "feature")
   111→        git_ops.delete_branch(repo, "old-feature", force=True)
   112→        assert (repo, "feature") in git_ops.checked_out_branches
   113→        assert "old-feature" in git_ops.deleted_branches
   114→    """
   115→
   116→    def __init__(
   117→        self,
   118→        *,
   119→        worktrees: dict[Path, list[WorktreeInfo]] | None = None,
   120→        current_branches: dict[Path, str | None] | None = None,
   121→        default_branches: dict[Path, str] | None = None,
   122→        trunk_branches: dict[Path, str] | None = None,
   123→        git_common_dirs: dict[Path, Path] | None = None,
   124→        branch_heads: dict[str, str] | None = None,
   125→        commit_messages: dict[str, str] | None = None,
   126→        staged_repos: set[Path] | None = None,
   127→        file_statuses: dict[Path, tuple[list[str], list[str], list[str]]] | None = None,
   128→        ahead_behind: dict[tuple[Path, str], tuple[int, int]] | None = None,
   129→        behind_commit_authors: dict[tuple[Path, str], list[str]] | None = None,
   130→        branch_sync_info: dict[Path, dict[str, BranchSyncInfo]] | None = None,
   131→        recent_commits: dict[Path, list[dict[str, str]]] | None = None,
   132→        recent_commits_by_branch: dict[tuple[Path, str], list[dict[str, str]]] | None = None,
   133→        existing_paths: set[Path] | None = None,
   134→        file_contents: dict[Path, str] | None = None,
   135→        delete_branch_raises: dict[str, Exception] | None = None,
   136→        local_branches: dict[Path, list[str]] | None = None,
   137→        remote_branches: dict[Path, list[str]] | None = None,
   138→        tracking_branch_failures: dict[str, str] | None = None,
   139→        dirty_worktrees: set[Path] | None = None,
   140→        branch_last_commit_times: dict[tuple[Path, str, str], str | None] | None = None,
   141→        repository_roots: dict[Path, Path] | None = None,
   142→        diff_to_branch: dict[tuple[Path, str], str] | None = None,
   143→        merge_conflicts: dict[tuple[str, str], bool] | None = None,
   144→        commits_ahead: dict[tuple[Path, str], int] | None = None,
   145→        commits_behind: dict[tuple[Path, str], int] | None = None,
   146→        remote_urls: dict[tuple[Path, str], str] | None = None,
   147→        add_all_raises: Exception | None = None,
   148→        fetch_branch_raises: Exception | None = None,
   149→        pull_branch_raises: Exception | None = None,
   150→        conflicted_files: list[str] | None = None,
   151→        rebase_in_progress: bool | Callable[[Path], bool] = False,
   152→        rebase_continue_raises: Exception | None = None,
   153→        rebase_continue_clears_rebase: bool = False,
   154→        commit_messages_since: dict[tuple[Path, str], list[str]] | None = None,
   155→        head_commit_messages_full: dict[Path, str] | None = None,
   156→        git_user_name: str | None = None,
   157→        branch_commits_with_authors: dict[tuple[Path, str, str, int], list[dict[str, str]]]
   158→        | None = None,
   159→        push_to_remote_error: PushError | None = None,
   160→        existing_tags: set[str] | None = None,
   161→        branch_divergence: dict[tuple[Path, str, str], BranchDivergence] | None = None,
   162→        rebase_onto_result: RebaseResult | None = None,
   163→        rebase_abort_raises: Exception | None = None,
   164→        pull_rebase_error: PullRebaseError | None = None,
   165→        merge_bases: dict[tuple[str, str], str] | None = None,
   166→        create_branch_error: BranchAlreadyExists | None = None,
   167→        add_worktree_error: str | None = None,
   168→        remove_worktree_error: str | None = None,
   169→        ahead_behind_raises: RuntimeError | None = None,
   170→    ) -> None:
   171→        """Create FakeGit with pre-configured state.
   172→
   173→        Args:
   174→            worktrees: Mapping of repo_root -> list of worktrees
   175→            current_branches: Mapping of cwd -> current branch
   176→            default_branches: Mapping of repo_root -> default branch
   177→            trunk_branches: Mapping of repo_root -> trunk branch name
   178→            git_common_dirs: Mapping of cwd -> git common directory
   179→            branch_heads: Mapping of branch name -> commit SHA
   180→            commit_messages: Mapping of commit SHA -> commit message
   181→            staged_repos: Set of repo roots that should report staged changes
   182→            file_statuses: Mapping of cwd -> (staged, modified, untracked) files
   183→            ahead_behind: Mapping of (cwd, branch) -> (ahead, behind) counts
   184→            behind_commit_authors: Mapping of (cwd, branch) -> list of author names
   185→                for commits on remote but not locally
   186→            branch_sync_info: Mapping of repo_root -> dict of branch name -> BranchSyncInfo
   187→            recent_commits: Mapping of cwd -> list of commit info dicts
   188→            recent_commits_by_branch: Mapping of (cwd, branch) -> list of commit info
   189→                dicts for branch-specific recent commits queries
   190→            existing_paths: Set of paths that should be treated as existing (for pure mode)
   191→            file_contents: Mapping of path -> file content (for commands that read files)
   192→            delete_branch_raises: Mapping of branch name -> exception to raise on delete
   193→            local_branches: Mapping of repo_root -> list of local branch names
   194→            remote_branches: Mapping of repo_root -> list of remote branch names
   195→                (with prefix like 'origin/branch-name')
   196→            tracking_branch_failures: Mapping of branch name -> error message to raise
   197→                when create_tracking_branch is called for that branch
   198→            dirty_worktrees: Set of worktree paths that have uncommitted/staged/untracked changes
   199→            branch_last_commit_times: Mapping of (repo_root, branch, trunk) -> ISO 8601 timestamp
   200→            repository_roots: Mapping of cwd -> repository root path
   201→            diff_to_branch: Mapping of (cwd, branch) -> diff output
   202→            merge_conflicts: Mapping of (base_branch, head_branch) -> has conflicts bool
   203→            commits_ahead: Mapping of (cwd, base_branch) -> commit count
   204→            commits_behind: Mapping of (cwd, target_branch) -> commit count
   205→            remote_urls: Mapping of (repo_root, remote_name) -> remote URL
   206→            add_all_raises: Exception to raise when add_all() is called
   207→            fetch_branch_raises: Exception to raise when fetch_branch() is called
   208→            pull_branch_raises: Exception to raise when pull_branch() is called
   209→            conflicted_files: List of file paths with merge conflicts
   210→            rebase_in_progress: Whether a rebase is currently in progress.
   211→                Can be a bool or a callable(cwd) -> bool for dynamic behavior.
   212→            rebase_continue_raises: Exception to raise when rebase_continue() is called
   213→            rebase_continue_clears_rebase: If True, rebase_continue() clears the rebase state
   214→            commit_messages_since: Mapping of (cwd, base_branch) -> list of commit messages
   215→            head_commit_messages_full: Mapping of cwd -> full commit message for HEAD
   216→            git_user_name: Configured git user.name to return from get_git_user_name()
   217→            branch_commits_with_authors: Mapping of (repo_root, branch, trunk, limit)
   218→                -> commit dicts with keys: sha, author, timestamp
   219→            push_to_remote_error: PushError to return when push_to_remote() is called
   220→            existing_tags: Set of tag names that exist in the repository
   221→            branch_divergence: Mapping of (cwd, branch, remote) -> BranchDivergence
   222→                for is_branch_diverged_from_remote()
   223→            rebase_onto_result: Result to return from rebase_onto(). Defaults to success.
   224→            rebase_abort_raises: Exception to raise when rebase_abort() is called
   225→            pull_rebase_error: PullRebaseError to return when pull_rebase() is called
   226→            merge_bases: Mapping of (ref1, ref2) -> merge base commit SHA for
   227→                get_merge_base(). Keys are ordered pairs, so (A, B) and (B, A)
   228→                are both checked.
   229→            create_branch_error: BranchAlreadyExists to return from create_branch()
   230→            add_worktree_error: Error message to raise as RuntimeError from add_worktree()
   231→            remove_worktree_error: Error message to raise as RuntimeError from remove_worktree()
   232→            ahead_behind_raises: If set, get_ahead_behind() raises this error
   233→        """
   234→        self._worktrees = worktrees or {}
   235→        self._current_branches = current_branches or {}
   236→        self._default_branches = default_branches or {}
   237→        self._trunk_branches = trunk_branches or {}
   238→        self._git_common_dirs = git_common_dirs or {}
   239→        self._branch_heads = branch_heads or {}
   240→        self._commit_messages = commit_messages or {}
   241→        self._repos_with_staged_changes: set[Path] = staged_repos or set()
   242→        self._file_statuses = file_statuses or {}
   243→        self._ahead_behind = ahead_behind or {}
   244→        self._behind_commit_authors = behind_commit_authors or {}
   245→        self._branch_sync_info = branch_sync_info or {}
   246→        self._recent_commits = recent_commits or {}
   247→        self._recent_commits_by_branch = recent_commits_by_branch or {}
   248→        self._existing_paths = existing_paths or set()
   249→        self._file_contents = file_contents or {}
   250→        self._delete_branch_raises = delete_branch_raises or {}
   251→        self._local_branches = local_branches or {}
   252→        self._remote_branches = remote_branches or {}
   253→        self._tracking_branch_failures = tracking_branch_failures or {}
   254→        self._dirty_worktrees = dirty_worktrees or set()
   255→        self._branch_last_commit_times = branch_last_commit_times or {}
   256→        self._repository_roots = repository_roots or {}
   257→        self._diff_to_branch = diff_to_branch or {}
   258→        self._merge_conflicts = merge_conflicts or {}
   259→        self._commits_ahead = commits_ahead or {}
   260→        self._commits_behind: dict[tuple[Path, str], int] = commits_behind or {}
   261→        self._remote_urls = remote_urls or {}
   262→        self._add_all_raises = add_all_raises
   263→        self._fetch_branch_raises = fetch_branch_raises
   264→        self._pull_branch_raises = pull_branch_raises
   265→        self._conflicted_files = conflicted_files or []
   266→        self._rebase_in_progress = rebase_in_progress
   267→        self._rebase_continue_raises = rebase_continue_raises
   268→        self._rebase_continue_clears_rebase = rebase_continue_clears_rebase
   269→        self._commit_messages_since = commit_messages_since or {}
   270→        self._head_commit_messages_full = head_commit_messages_full or {}
   271→        self._git_user_name = git_user_name
   272→        self._branch_commits_with_authors = branch_commits_with_authors or {}
   273→        self._push_to_remote_error = push_to_remote_error
   274→        self._existing_tags: set[str] = existing_tags or set()
   275→        self._branch_divergence = branch_divergence or {}
   276→        self._rebase_onto_result = rebase_onto_result
   277→        self._rebase_abort_raises = rebase_abort_raises
   278→        self._pull_rebase_error = pull_rebase_error
   279→        self._merge_bases = merge_bases or {}
   280→        self._remove_worktree_error = remove_worktree_error
   281→
   282→        # Mutation tracking
   283→        self._deleted_branches: list[str] = []
   284→        self._checked_out_branches: list[tuple[Path, str]] = []
   285→        self._detached_checkouts: list[tuple[Path, str]] = []
   286→        self._fetched_branches: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
   287→        self._pulled_branches: list[tuple[str, str, bool]] = []
   288→        self._created_tracking_branches: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
   289→        self._staged_files: list[str] = []
   290→        self._commits: list[CommitRecord] = []
   291→        self._branch_commits: list[BranchCommitRecord] = []
   292→        self._pushed_branches: list[PushedBranch] = []
   293→        self._created_branches: list[
   294→            tuple[Path, str, str, bool]
   295→        ] = []  # (cwd, branch_name, start_point, force)
   296→        self._rebase_continue_calls: list[Path] = []
   297→        self._config_settings: list[tuple[str, str, str]] = []  # (key, value, scope)
   298→        self._created_tags: list[tuple[str, str]] = []  # (tag_name, message)
   299→        self._pushed_tags: list[tuple[str, str]] = []  # (remote, tag_name)
   300→        self._rebase_onto_calls: list[tuple[Path, str]] = []  # (cwd, target_ref)
   301→        self._rebase_abort_calls: list[Path] = []
   302→        self._pull_rebase_calls: list[tuple[Path, str, str]] = []  # (cwd, remote, branch)
   303→        self._config_sets_records: list[ConfigSetRecord] = []
   304→
   305→        # Repo operations subgateway
   306→        self._repo_gateway = FakeGitRepoOps()
   307→        self._repo_gateway.link_state(
   308→            repository_roots=self._repository_roots,
   309→            git_common_dirs=self._git_common_dirs,
   310→            worktrees=self._worktrees,
   311→        )
   312→
   313→        # Analysis operations subgateway
   314→        self._analysis_gateway = FakeGitAnalysisOps()
   315→        self._analysis_gateway.link_state(
   316→            commits_ahead=self._commits_ahead,
   317→            commits_behind=self._commits_behind,
   318→            merge_bases=self._merge_bases,
   319→            diffs=self._diff_to_branch,
   320→        )
   321→
   322→        # Config operations subgateway
   323→        self._config_gateway = FakeGitConfigOps(git_user_name=git_user_name)
   324→        self._config_gateway.link_state(
   325→            user_names={},
   326→            config_values={},
   327→            git_user_name=git_user_name,
   328→        )
   329→        self._config_gateway.link_mutation_tracking(
   330→            config_sets=self._config_sets_records,
   331→        )
   332→
   333→        # Worktree subgateway
   334→        self._worktree_gateway = FakeWorktree(
   335→            worktrees=self._worktrees,
   336→            existing_paths=self._existing_paths,
   337→            dirty_worktrees=self._dirty_worktrees,
   338→            add_worktree_error=add_worktree_error,
   339→            remove_worktree_error=self._remove_worktree_error,
   340→        )
   341→
   342→        # Branch operations subgateway - linked to FakeGit's state
   343→        self._branch_gateway = FakeGitBranchOps(
   344→            worktrees=self._worktrees,
   345→            current_branches=self._current_branches,
   346→            local_branches=self._local_branches,
   347→            remote_branches=self._remote_branches,
   348→            branch_heads=self._branch_heads,
   349→            trunk_branches=self._trunk_branches,
   350→            ahead_behind=self._ahead_behind,
   351→            branch_divergence=self._branch_divergence,
   352→            branch_sync_info=self._branch_sync_info,
   353→            behind_commit_authors=self._behind_commit_authors,
   354→            branch_last_commit_times=self._branch_last_commit_times,
   355→            branch_commits_with_authors=self._branch_commits_with_authors,
   356→            delete_branch_raises=self._delete_branch_raises,
   357→            tracking_branch_failures=self._tracking_branch_failures,
   358→            create_branch_error=create_branch_error,
   359→            ahead_behind_raises=ahead_behind_raises,
   360→        )
   361→        # Link mutation tracking so FakeGit properties see mutations from FakeGitBranchOps
   362→        self._branch_gateway.link_mutation_tracking(
   363→            created_branches=self._created_branches,
   364→            deleted_branches=self._deleted_branches,
   365→            checked_out_branches=self._checked_out_branches,
   366→            detached_checkouts=self._detached_checkouts,
   367→            created_tracking_branches=self._created_tracking_branches,
   368→        )
   369→
   370→        # Remote operations subgateway - linked to FakeGit's state
   371→        self._remote_gateway = FakeGitRemoteOps(
   372→            remote_urls=self._remote_urls,
   373→            fetch_branch_raises=self._fetch_branch_raises,
   374→            pull_branch_raises=self._pull_branch_raises,
   375→            push_to_remote_error=self._push_to_remote_error,
   376→            pull_rebase_error=self._pull_rebase_error,
   377→        )
   378→        # Link mutation tracking so FakeGit properties see mutations from FakeGitRemoteOps
   379→        self._remote_gateway.link_mutation_tracking(
   380→            fetched_branches=self._fetched_branches,
   381→            pulled_branches=self._pulled_branches,
   382→            pushed_branches=self._pushed_branches,
   383→            pull_rebase_calls=self._pull_rebase_calls,
   384→        )
   385→
   386→        # Commit operations subgateway - linked to FakeGit's state
   387→        self._commit_gateway = FakeGitCommitOps(
   388→            commit_messages=self._commit_messages,
   389→            recent_commits=self._recent_commits,
   390→            recent_commits_by_branch=self._recent_commits_by_branch,
   391→            commit_messages_since=self._commit_messages_since,
   392→            head_commit_messages_full=self._head_commit_messages_full,
   393→            commits_ahead=self._commits_ahead,
   394→            add_all_raises=self._add_all_raises,
   395→            dirty_worktrees=self._dirty_worktrees,
   396→        )
   397→        # Link mutation tracking so FakeGit properties see mutations from FakeGitCommitOps
   398→        self._commit_gateway.link_mutation_tracking(
   399→            staged_files=self._staged_files,
   400→            commits=self._commits,
   401→            branch_commits=self._branch_commits,
   402→        )
   403→        # Link mutable state so changes propagate back to FakeGit
   404→        self._commit_gateway.link_state(
   405→            commits_ahead=self._commits_ahead,
   406→            dirty_worktrees=self._dirty_worktrees,
   407→        )
   408→
   409→        # Status operations subgateway - linked to FakeGit's state
   410→        self._status_gateway = FakeGitStatusOps(
   411→            staged_repos=self._repos_with_staged_changes,
   412→            file_statuses=self._file_statuses,
   413→            merge_conflicts=self._merge_conflicts,
   414→            conflicted_files=self._conflicted_files,
   415→        )
   416→        # Link state so FakeGit modifications are visible to status subgateway
   417→        self._status_gateway.link_state(
   418→            staged_repos=self._repos_with_staged_changes,
   419→            file_statuses=self._file_statuses,
   420→            merge_conflicts=self._merge_conflicts,
   421→            conflicted_files=self._conflicted_files,
   422→        )
   423→
   424→        # Rebase operations subgateway - linked to FakeGit's state
   425→        self._rebase_gateway = FakeGitRebaseOps(
   426→            rebase_in_progress=rebase_in_progress,
   427→            rebase_onto_result=rebase_onto_result,
   428→            rebase_continue_raises=rebase_continue_raises,
   429→            rebase_continue_clears_rebase=rebase_continue_clears_rebase,
   430→            rebase_abort_raises=rebase_abort_raises,
   431→        )
   432→        # Link mutation tracking
   433→        self._rebase_gateway.link_mutation_tracking(
   434→            rebase_onto_calls=self._rebase_onto_calls,
   435→            rebase_continue_calls=self._rebase_continue_calls,
   436→            rebase_abort_calls=self._rebase_abort_calls,
   437→        )
   438→
   439→        # Tag operations subgateway - linked to FakeGit's state
   440→        self._tag_gateway = FakeGitTagOps(
   441→            existing_tags=self._existing_tags,
   442→        )
   443→        # Link mutation tracking so FakeGit properties see mutations from FakeGitTagOps
   444→        self._tag_gateway.link_mutation_tracking(
   445→            existing_tags=self._existing_tags,
   446→            created_tags=self._created_tags,
   447→            pushed_tags=self._pushed_tags,
   448→        )

   450→    @property

   507→
   508→    @property

   534→    @property

   543→    @property

   561→    @property

   588→    def read_file(self, path: Path) -> str:
   589→        """Read file content from in-memory store.
   590→
   591→        Used in erk_inmem_env for commands that need to read files
   592→        (e.g., plan files, config files) without actual filesystem I/O.
   593→
   594→        Raises:

   596→        """

   618→        Returns list of BranchCommitRecord objects with cwd, branch, files, and message.

   622→    @property

   631→    @property

   639→
   640→        Returns list of (tag_name, message) tuples.

   645→    @property

   656→        """Get list of pull_rebase calls for test assertions.

   662→

   671→
   672→    @property

   674→        """Get list of rebase_abort calls for test assertions.
   675→
   676→        This property is for test assertions only.

   678→        return self._rebase_gateway.rebase_abort_calls
   679→
   680→    @property
   681→    def rebase_continue_calls(self) -> list[Path]:
   682→        """Get list of rebase_continue calls for test assertions.
   683→
   684→        This property is for test assertions only.
   685→        """
   686→        return self._rebase_gateway.rebase_continue_calls
   687→
   688→    def create_linked_branch_ops(self) -> FakeGitBranchOps:
   689→        """Return the FakeGitBranchOps linked to this FakeGit's state.
   690→
   691→        The returned FakeGitBranchOps shares mutable state and mutation tracking
   692→        with this FakeGit instance. This allows tests to check FakeGit properties
   693→        like deleted_branches while mutations happen through BranchManager.
   694→
   695→        Returns:
   696→            FakeGitBranchOps with linked state and mutation tracking
   697→
   698→        Note:
   699→            This method now returns the same instance as self.branch, which is
   700→            already linked during __init__.
   701→        """
   702→        return self._branch_gateway
